title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Program Brief: Partial Token State
summary: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the partial token state increment in bounded streaming analyzer. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-partial-token-state — Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-streaming-bounded-memory-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_streaming_bounded_memory_4(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

# Evidence: bookbot-partial-token-state-1yksc9
# Competency: bookbot-partial-token-state.
# Case 1385: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.

def evidence_bookbot_partial_token_state_1yksc9(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1yksc9 = "1yksc9-9csky1-1385"
    assert evidence_variant_1yksc9
    evidence_axes_1yksc9 = (
        "corpus1yksc9",
        "policy9csky1",
        "revision1385",
        "command1yksc91385",
        "report9csky11385",
        "test13851yksc9",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1yksc9)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"

Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Partial Token State model · Theory Partial Token State, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Each chunk can be tokenized independently without affecting results.
